text.title: Accessibility Automation QA Procedure
text.summary: 4-step procedure to apply Accessibility Automation QA end-to-end. Each step has named input, action, and output. Decision gates reference rules from `01-core-rules.xml`.

step.name: define-wcag-floor-and-tooling
step.input: WCAG version (2.1 AA), tool (axe-core, Pa11y, Lighthouse), CI target
step.action: Pin the WCAG level + the tool versions in repo. Decide which violations block the build vs ticket-only.

step.name: instrument-key-flows
step.action: Add a11y automation tests for each critical flow (named exit_criterion: zero blocking violations on the flow).
step.output: A11y test bundle wired into CI

step.name: review-violations-with-owner
step.action: Owner triages each new violation; severity is decided against the WCAG conformance matrix; deviations are logged.

step.name: publish-runbook
step.action: Generate the a11y-runbook with common patterns + fixes; named owner signs off; runbook is referenced from CI alert URLs.

section.title: Decision gates
section.p: Between steps, consult `content/06-decision-tree.xml` if the observed signal does not match expected output. The decision tree always terminates at a rule id from `01-core-rules.xml` or at `skip-this-methodology`.
